evals.prompt:
Write a clinical study report from these survived findings, following the ICH E3 CSR skeleton and situating it in the CTD module frame. Study: 'a phase III trial of an oral antihypertensive'. Include all required sections in order. Write the report to clinical-report.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_cli_1 | title: The primary objective was to demonstrate superiority in mean systolic blood pressure reduction at 12 weeks versus placebo | dimension: objectives | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/trial-objectives | author: Protocol | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_cli_2 | title: The trial used a randomized double-blind placebo-controlled parallel-group design with 1,200 participants | dimension: design | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/trial-design | author: Investigational Plan | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_cli_3 | title: The treatment arm achieved a 9.4 mmHg greater systolic reduction than placebo (95% CI 7.1 to 11.7) | dimension: efficacy | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/efficacy-result | author: Results | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_cli_4 | title: Adverse events were predominantly mild dizziness, with no excess serious adverse events versus placebo | dimension: safety | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/safety-result | author: Safety Analysis | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A clinical study report following ICH E3 order: Synopsis, Ethics, Investigators/Structure, Objectives, Investigational Plan, Methods (efficacy & safety), Results, Discussion & Conclusions, Tables/Figures/Appendices. The report situates itself in the CTD five-module frame and notes that an E3 CSR lives in Module 5. Objectives precede Methods which precede Results. Efficacy and safety reported distinctly. No raw urn:mif: identifiers in prose. Scope caveat stated.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Required Synopsis section present
Required Ethics section present
Required Investigators & Study Structure section present
Required Objectives section present
Required Investigational Plan section present
Required Methods (efficacy & safety) section present
Required Results section present
Required Discussion & Conclusions section present
Required Tables, Figures & Appendices section present
Scope caveat stated (genre reproduces E3 structure, not clinical validity / regulatory acceptance)
CTD-module framing present (E3 CSR situated in Module 5)
ICH E3 ordering: Objectives precede Methods precede Results
At least one citation URL from the MIF findings appears as a reference
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose or body text
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: Efficacy and safety results are reported distinctly
expectations.criteria: The Results section reports the efficacy outcome (9.4 mmHg, 95% CI) and the safety outcome (mild dizziness, no excess SAEs) as separate, clearly labeled results rather than merged.

expectations.description: The report situates the CSR in the CTD Module 5 frame
expectations.criteria: The report names the CTD five-module structure and states that an ICH E3 clinical study report belongs to Module 5 (clinical study reports).

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or f_cli_ internal IDs appear as visible prose
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_cli_' must not appear as visible body text; internal IDs resolve to scientific/regulatory references.

evals.prompt:
Produce a clinical study report on 'a phase II oncology trial'. The finding set includes one falsified finding and one weakened finding — handle each according to the genre rules. Write the report to clinical-report.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_onc_1 | title: The primary endpoint was objective response rate by RECIST 1.1 at 24 weeks | dimension: objectives | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/onc-endpoint | author: Protocol | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_onc_2 | title: The therapy cured 100% of patients with no recurrence ever observed in any subgroup | dimension: efficacy | verdict: falsified | citation: https://example.com/cure-claim | author: Sponsor Release | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_onc_3 | title: A subgroup signal suggested longer progression-free survival in PD-L1-high patients, though the subgroup was underpowered | dimension: efficacy | verdict: weakened | citation: https://example.com/subgroup-signal | author: Exploratory Analysis | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A CSR that (1) excludes the falsified 100%-cure claim (f_onc_2), (2) includes the weakened subgroup signal (f_onc_3) with an explicit uncertainty qualifier (underpowered/exploratory), (3) uses the survived endpoint definition (f_onc_1), (4) keeps ICH E3 section ordering and CTD framing.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Objectives section present
Results section present
Survived finding f_onc_1 (RECIST endpoint) appears in the report
Weakened finding f_onc_3 (subgroup signal) appears with annotation (weakened = include, not exclude)
CTD-module framing present (E3 CSR situated in Module 5)
ICH E3 ordering: Objectives precede Methods precede Results
Falsified finding f_onc_2 source (example.com/cure-claim) is NOT cited
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: The falsified 100%-cure claim does NOT appear as a result
expectations.criteria: The claim that the therapy cured 100% of patients (Sponsor Release 2025) must not appear as an efficacy result. It may be referenced only when describing the verification/exclusion policy.

expectations.description: The weakened subgroup signal carries an explicit uncertainty qualifier
expectations.criteria: The PD-L1-high subgroup signal is presented with a qualifier such as 'underpowered', 'exploratory', or 'hypothesis-generating' — not asserted as a confirmed efficacy result.

evals.prompt:
Write a clinical study report from these findings, following ICH E3 order with CTD framing. Study: 'a vaccine immunogenicity trial'. All four findings survived. Citations are mandatory; every claim must trace to a finding. Write the report to clinical-report.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_vac_1 | title: The study received ethics-committee approval and all participants provided written informed consent | dimension: ethics | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/ethics-approval | author: Ethics Section | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_vac_2 | title: The primary objective was geometric mean antibody titer at day 28 after a two-dose schedule | dimension: objectives | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/vac-objective | author: Protocol | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_vac_3 | title: Seroconversion reached 94% in the per-protocol population at day 28 | dimension: efficacy | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/seroconversion | author: Results | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_vac_4 | title: Reactogenicity was mostly mild injection-site pain resolving within 48 hours | dimension: safety | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/reactogenicity | author: Safety Analysis | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A complete CSR with all ICH E3 sections in order and CTD framing. Every claim traces to one of the four findings with a scientific/regulatory reference. The Ethics section reflects the approval and consent finding. No uncited assertions. No urn:mif: leakage in prose.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Synopsis section present
Ethics section present
Objectives section present
Methods section present
Results section present
Discussion & Conclusions section present
ICH E3 ordering: Objectives precede Methods precede Results
CTD-module framing present (E3 CSR situated in Module 5)
At least one citation URL from the MIF findings appears as a reference
Finding f_vac_1 (ethics approval & consent) referenced
Finding f_vac_3 (seroconversion) referenced
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the body text
No internal MIF finding ID handles leak into the output body

expectations.description: Every claim traces to a supplied finding with a reference
expectations.criteria: Each statement cites the named source from the findings (Ethics Section, Protocol, Results, Safety Analysis) with its URL. No uncited assertions.

expectations.description: The Discussion separates immunogenicity benefit from reactogenicity
expectations.criteria: The Discussion & Conclusions section weighs the immunogenicity result (94% seroconversion) against the reactogenicity/safety profile, rather than restating results only.
